evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/httpx. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/httpx --head qlty-setup --base master
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Python project using ruff. Detects existing pyproject.toml with ruff config. Enables ruff, trufflehog, markdownlint, yamllint, actionlint. Asks user about modes and smells before committing.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/httpx
PR branch is qlty-setup
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
ruff plugin is enabled (Python project)
trufflehog plugin is enabled and set to block mode
markdownlint is enabled (markdown files present)
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
Detected existing pyproject.toml with ruff configuration and referenced it via config_files or noted it is auto-detected
Did NOT enable flake8 or black (ruff covers both)
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream encode/httpx repo

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/requests. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/requests --head qlty-setup --base main
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Python project. Enables ruff (or flake8 if no ruff config found), bandit for security, trufflehog, osv-scanner for dependencies. Asks the user about security plugin modes.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/requests
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
A Python linter (ruff or flake8) is enabled
trufflehog is enabled and set to block
osv-scanner or trivy is enabled for dependency scanning
bandit is enabled or recommended for this security-sensitive HTTP library
Smells configuration is discussed with the user before committing
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream psf/requests repo

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/zod. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/zod --head qlty-setup --base main
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a TypeScript project. Detects existing ESLint config and references it via config_files. Enables eslint, tsc, prettier, markdownlint, trufflehog. Asks about extra_packages needed for the ESLint setup.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/zod
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
eslint plugin is enabled
tsc plugin is enabled (TypeScript project with tsconfig.json)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block
prettier is enabled
Skill detected the existing ESLint config file and proposed config_files or extra_packages
markdownlint is enabled
Did not enable eslint AND biome simultaneously
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream colinhacks/zod repo

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/shoelace. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/shoelace --head qlty-setup --base next
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a TypeScript + CSS web components project. Enables eslint, stylelint (for CSS/SCSS), prettier, trufflehog. If stylelint config is found, references it via config_files. Proposes extra_packages for any stylelint extends detected.

.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
eslint plugin is enabled
stylelint plugin is enabled (CSS/TypeScript project)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block
prettier is enabled
If stylelint config file is found, config_files is set or extra_packages proposes any detected stylelint extends
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream shoelace-style/shoelace repo

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/got. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/got --head qlty-setup --base main
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a TypeScript/JavaScript project with minimal existing linter setup. Enables eslint, prettier, markdownlint, trufflehog, actionlint. Runs qlty init if .qlty/ does not exist. Interactively asks about smells and modes.

.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
eslint or oxc plugin is enabled (TypeScript/JS project)
prettier is enabled
trufflehog is enabled and set to block
markdownlint is enabled
Skill ran qlty init or wrote .qlty/qlty.toml from scratch
Did not enable tsc without confirming tsconfig.json compiles the project
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream sindresorhus/got repo

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/puma. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/puma --head qlty-setup --base main
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for a Ruby project. Detects existing .rubocop.yml and references it. Enables rubocop, trufflehog, markdownlint, yamllint. Does not enable standardrb (rubocop config present). Discusses smells for Ruby.

rubocop plugin is enabled
Skill detected the existing .rubocop.yml and referenced it via config_files
trufflehog is enabled and set to block
markdownlint is enabled

Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream puma/puma repo

knip is proposed or discussed (unused dependency detection in monorepos)
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream changesets/changesets repo

evals.prompt: IMPORTANT: This repo is a fork at laura-mlg/cli. When creating the PR, use: gh pr create --repo laura-mlg/cli --head qlty-setup --base trunk
evals.expected_output: PR created with .qlty/qlty.toml for the GitHub CLI (Go + Docker + GitHub Actions). Enables golangci-lint or gofmt, hadolint (Dockerfiles), actionlint (workflows), zizmor (GitHub Actions security), trufflehog, markdownlint, yamllint. Security plugins are foregrounded given the security-sensitive nature of this tool.
expectations: Created a PR on laura-mlg/cli
PR base branch is trunk (not main or master)
.qlty/qlty.toml is committed in the PR
A Go linter is enabled (gofmt or golangci-lint)
hadolint is enabled (Dockerfiles present)
actionlint is enabled (GitHub Actions workflows present)
zizmor is enabled or strongly proposed (security-sensitive tool with many GH Actions workflows)
trufflehog is enabled and set to block
markdownlint is enabled
yamllint is enabled
Security plugins are discussed with appropriate rigor given this is a security-sensitive CLI tool
Did not create .qlty/qlty.toml in the upstream cli/cli repo
